topic: SEC-6.4 rewrite: WIN-024 taxonomy fix + relabeling defense paragraph (EXP-067)
our_argument_summary: SEC-6.4 'Globe Values Relabeled as Dome Predictions' was rewritten via EXP-067 to fix two issues: (1) ISS-711 — WIN-024 taxonomy mismatch (relabels_standard=false WIN placed in relabeling section), fixed by changing subheading to 'false causal attribution' with explicit tag note; (2) ISS-712 — defense_survives=3 'relabeling proves too much' objection, fixed by adding a full paragraph directly addressing the strongest rebuttal ('if standard physics explains the same observations, isn't that what any good theory should do?'). The section now has four example domains (tidal, Hubble, P-wave, Roaring 40s), a defense paragraph, and computed placeholders for relabeling counts.

holes_found.description: INTERNAL LATITUDE CONTRADICTION between WIN-024 detail_evidence and SEC-6.4 text. WIN-024's detail_evidence states: 'the standard IGRF/WMM reference (32,000 nT at sea level) places [the SAA southern boundary] at ~45–50°S, which does overlap the Roaring 40s. This latitude coincidence is real — the dome is not fabricating a nonexistent overlap.' The new SEC-6.4 text from EXP-067 states: 'The spatial overlap is approximate (SAA southern boundary ~30–35°S vs Roaring 40s ~40–50°S) and non-discriminating.' These are contradictory claims about the same geographic fact. WIN-024 says the SAA boundary is at 45-50°S and the overlap IS real; SEC-6.4 says the boundary is at 30-35°S and the overlap is only approximate. A dome defender could compile both passages and say: 'Your review can't even agree on where the SAA boundary is. Your WIN-024 analysis says the overlap is real; your section summary says it isn't. Which is it?' This is exactly the kind of self-contradiction we criticize the dome model for.
holes_found.recommendation: Align the SEC-6.4 WIN-024 paragraph with the WIN-024 detail_evidence. The WIN-024 approach is stronger: acknowledge the latitude overlap is real (it is, at the 32,000 nT contour), then destroy the causal claim on longitude grounds (Roaring 40s span all longitudes, SAA spans only ~130° of longitude). Change SEC-6.4 text from 'The spatial overlap is approximate (SAA southern boundary ~30–35°S vs Roaring 40s ~40–50°S)' to something like: 'The latitude overlap is real — the SAA southern boundary at the 32,000 nT contour (~45–50°S) does overlap the Roaring 40s belt (~40–50°S). But latitude coincidence is the weakest possible evidence for a causal claim. The decisive test is longitude: the Roaring 40s encircle the entire Southern Hemisphere; the SAA spans only ~130° of longitude.'

holes_found.description: HARDCODED COUNT '21 WINs' in the defense paragraph. The text states 'The 21 WINs that do not relabel standard physics are analyzed individually in Part 3.' This is hardcoded (TOTAL_WINS - CA_RELABELS = 70 - 49 = 21). The EXP-067 expansion JSON claimed it would add a {{CA_NONRELABEL}} placeholder, but no such placeholder exists in the build pipeline (generate-html.js computes {{CA_RELABELS}} but not {{CA_NONRELABEL}}). If a new WIN is onboarded with relabels_standard=true, this count becomes stale. The CLAUDE.md explicitly states: 'Never hardcode a number that can be derived from the data — we criticize the dome model for doing exactly that.'
holes_found.recommendation: Either: (a) add a {{CA_NONRELABEL}} placeholder to the build pipeline (generate-html.js) computed as total_reviewed - relabels_standard, then use it in sections.json; or (b) rewrite the sentence to avoid the specific count: 'The remaining WINs — those that do not relabel standard physics — are analyzed individually in Part 3.' Option (b) is simpler and still communicates the point.

holes_found.description: The WIN-024 paragraph says the dome 'provides no mechanism by which geomagnetic field intensity would drive atmospheric circulation.' This is accurate but slightly uncharitable. The dome model's claim is not that the SAA drives the Roaring 40s via geomagnetic field intensity per se, but that both phenomena arise from the same 'aetheric rim pressure at the disc edge' (a dome-specific mechanism). The current phrasing strawmans slightly by attributing a specific mechanism (geomagnetic → wind) that the dome doesn't actually claim. The real argument should be: the dome posits a shared cause (rim pressure) for two phenomena that have well-established independent causes — this is an unfounded causal bundling, not a mechanistic claim about B-field driving wind.
holes_found.recommendation: Reframe to: 'The dome posits that both phenomena arise from shared "aetheric rim pressure" at the disc edge, but provides no quantitative mechanism connecting geomagnetic and atmospheric phenomena — and the geographic mismatch (130° longitude SAA vs 360° winds) directly contradicts the shared-cause hypothesis.' This is harder to rebut because it engages with what the dome actually claims.

holes_found.description: The defense paragraph's final sentence uses the P-wave shadow zone as the 'strongest illustration' of why relabeling fails: 'the dome's proposed mechanism is not merely redundant with standard physics — it is geometrically incompatible with the azimuthal symmetry of the observed shadow zone.' This is strong, but WIN-064 is categorized as 'Std Model Explains' (relabels_standard=true), NOT as 'Self-Contradicted.' The geometric incompatibility argument actually sounds more like a self-contradiction (the dome's own mechanism contradicts the observation it claims to explain). Using a 'Std Model Explains' WIN as your exemplar of geometric incompatibility creates a subtle tension — if the mechanism is truly geometrically incompatible, why isn't it self-contradicted or refuted by data?
holes_found.recommendation: Consider either: (a) noting explicitly that WIN-064's relabeling verdict was chosen because the primary pattern is vocabulary substitution (renaming 'liquid core refraction' as 'Sub-Terrestrial Wall'), with the geometric incompatibility as a secondary aggravating factor; or (b) using a different exemplar for the defense paragraph that doesn't raise verdict-category questions. The tidal WINs are actually a cleaner example: they are self-contradicted AND relabeling, and the Section 2.4 cross-reference makes the argument airtight.

stronger_arguments.description: The defense paragraph could be strengthened by citing the devastating derives_from_dome statistic: of 49 WINs tagged relabels_standard=true, ZERO also have derives_from_dome=true. In the entire model, only 2 out of 70 WINs derive their predicted values from dome geometry. This transforms the argument from 'they explain the same thing differently' to 'they cannot produce ANY of these values from their own framework — the dome framework is parasitic on standard physics in 97% of its predictions.' This single statistic is arguably the most powerful quantitative summary of the entire review.
sources: data/wins.json code_analysis tags: relabels_standard=true AND derives_from_dome=true count = 0; total derives_from_dome=true = 2/70

stronger_arguments.description: The defense paragraph acknowledges 'reproducing known results IS a minimum bar for any scientific theory' — good. But it could go further by naming the specific standard: Lakatos's 'novel facts' criterion. A research programme earns its keep by predicting novel facts, not by re-explaining known ones. The dome model fails this criterion: its relabeling WINs reproduce known values, and its non-relabeling WINs are refuted/self-contradicted/unfalsifiable. Citing Lakatos makes the philosophical framework explicit and harder to dismiss as mere opinion.
sources: Lakatos (1978) 'The Methodology of Scientific Research Programmes'

kernel_of_truth.description: The defense paragraph is genuinely well-executed. It does something rare in adversarial writing: it acknowledges the strongest version of the opponent's objection ('any good theory should explain the same observations') before dismantling it on the specific grounds of derivation vs importation. The distinction between 'legitimate re-explanation' and 'hollow relabeling' is philosophically sound and precisely targeted. The WIN-024 taxonomy fix (calling it 'false causal attribution') is also correct — it accurately reflects the code_analysis tags and avoids the previous categorical confusion.
kernel_of_truth.why_it_doesnt_save_claim: The execution has two significant flaws: the latitude contradiction with WIN-024's own detail text (which a defender WILL find), and the hardcoded '21' count (which violates the project's own anti-hardcoding principle and will become stale). The latitude issue is particularly dangerous because it's the exact kind of self-contradiction we criticize the dome model for — a defender could quote both passages side by side and say 'physician, heal thyself.'

text_fingerprint.section_title: 6.4 Globe Values Relabeled as Dome Predictions

ISS-711.detail: WIN-024 subheading changed from 'related pattern, false correlation' to 'false causal attribution' with explicit note about code_analysis tag agreement. Clear, accurate, and defensible.

ISS-712.detail: Defense paragraph directly addresses the 'relabeling proves too much' objection with genuine philosophical rigor. However, introduces a new latitude inconsistency with WIN-024 detail text that partially undermines the WIN-024 example within the section.

advocate_mode.best_defense: Your own document can't agree on whether the SAA and Roaring 40s overlap. Your WIN-024 detail says the SAA boundary at 32,000 nT is 45-50°S and that 'this latitude coincidence is real.' Your Section 6.4 summary says the SAA boundary is 30-35°S and calls the overlap merely 'approximate.' If you can't get your own facts straight on a basic geographic question, why should I trust your analysis of complex electromagnetic phenomena? And as for your 'relabeling' critique — you say reproducing known results 'IS a minimum bar for any scientific theory,' then spend six paragraphs criticizing us for doing exactly that. Your defense paragraph essentially concedes our first point (we DO explain the same observations) and then invents a distinction ('deriving' vs 'importing') that applies equally to every theory in the history of science. Newton didn't 'derive' the gravitational constant G from first principles — he measured it. Are Newtonian predictions 'hollow relabeling' too?
advocate_mode.preemptive_recommendation: Fix the latitude contradiction (see major hole above) — this is the single most exploitable inconsistency. For the Newton/G objection: add a footnote or parenthetical noting that the relevant distinction is not whether a theory uses measured constants (all do) but whether those constants come from the theory's own framework or from the rival theory's framework. Newton's G was measured independently of any competing gravitational theory; the dome's H₀ = 70 km/s/Mpc comes directly from globe cosmology's distance ladder. The dome doesn't have its own measurement of H₀ — it imports the globe's.

cross_win_consistency.inconsistencies_found: LATITUDE CONTRADICTION: WIN-024 detail_evidence states SAA southern boundary at 32,000 nT contour is ~45-50°S and explicitly says 'This latitude coincidence is real.' SEC-6.4 rewrite states SAA southern boundary is ~30-35°S and implies the overlap is only approximate. These use different contour thresholds without noting the difference, and reach opposite conclusions about whether the overlap exists.

claims_checked: 49/70 = 70% relabels_standard (verified: 49 WINs have relabels_standard=true out of 70 total = 70.0%)
Tidal periods: M2 12.42 hr, S2 12.00 hr, K1 23.93 hr, O1 25.82 hr, N2 12.66 hr (these are standard tidal constants, correct)
quantitative_verification.computations: Verified relabeling count from wins.json: 49 relabels_standard=true, 21 false, 0 with no code_analysis. Verified Hubble length: c/H₀ = 299792.458/70 = 4282.75 Mpc ≈ 4283 Mpc. Confirmed derives_from_dome: 0 of 49 relabeling WINs derive from dome; only 2/70 total.
quantitative_verification.discrepancies: The '21' count is numerically correct NOW but is hardcoded rather than computed via placeholder, making it fragile to future WIN additions.

deeper_analysis: The EXP-067 rewrite is a net improvement over the previous version. Both ISS-711 and ISS-712 are addressed with genuine care and intellectual rigor. The defense paragraph is arguably the most philosophically sophisticated passage in the entire review — it grants the strongest version of the opponent's argument before dismantling it on precise grounds. However, the rewrite introduced one new problem (the latitude inconsistency) while fixing two old ones, and failed to implement the {{CA_NONRELABEL}} placeholder it promised. The latitude issue is the more urgent fix because it's an internal self-contradiction that a defender could exploit without needing any external knowledge — just quoting two sections of our own document side by side. The hardcoded count is a policy violation that will bite us the next time a WIN is onboarded. Overall assessment: the section is stronger than before but needs one more pass to align the WIN-024 geographic claims between the detail entry and the section text.

citations_verified: Part 2b cross-reference (code analysis section) — exists and contains relabeling data
Section 2.4 cross-reference (tidal pattern self-contradiction) — exists and is consistent
Laplace (1775) — general tidal theory attribution, directionally correct
George Darwin (1883) — harmonic tidal analysis, correct
